research_goal: Understand why PMs at early-stage startups struggle with discovery research.

themes.label: Synthesis, not scheduling, is the bottleneck

findings.text: The team can get customers on calls, but post-call synthesis is skipped because it takes too much time.

supporting_quotes.quote: The hard part is not getting calls. We can get customers on the phone.

themes.label: Question banks stay broad for too long

findings.text: By later calls, PMs should be testing sharper hypotheses, but they often continue asking generic workflow questions.

supporting_quotes.quote: By the fourth call we should be testing a sharper hypothesis.

findings.text: PMs may accept agent-generated questions if the system shows what changed, why, and which evidence supports the change.

supporting_quotes.quote: Show me what changed, why it changed, and what evidence it came from.

open_questions: Do other PMs also see synthesis as a bigger blocker than recruiting?
What evidence format makes an AI-generated question bank trustworthy?
How often do teams currently update interview guides between calls?
